title: We spent $0 on ads using AI. Here's our growth strategy
post:
Zero dollars on advertising. First 1,000 customers.

Our growth strategy wasn't clever content marketing or viral loops. It was AI-powered outbound at scale.

We built a system that identifies our ideal customer profile, researches their company and role, generates personalized cold outreach, and sends it across email, LinkedIn, and Twitter simultaneously.

Response rates are higher than traditional cold outreach because the messages actually feel human. The AI researched the prospect deeply before writing.

This is what AI enables: distribution that used to require a team of 10 SDRs, done by one person with good prompts.

The bottleneck for most startups isn't product anymore. It's reaching customers. AI removes that bottleneck.

What's your customer acquisition stack look like? Thoughts?
